input.background: Exercises 13.1, 13.3, and 13.5 considered birth weight in four groups of mothers. Table 13.3 lists the data. Data are also stored online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV. The ANOVA test found significant evidence against H0 (P=0.042). Now apply a Kruskal-Wallis test.
input.question: Calculate the test statistic for the Kruskal-Wallis test.

output.answer: Chi-square = 7.305 with 3 df (Calculated with SPSS, Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.).

meta_info.caption: Smoking and birth weight, Kruskal-Wallis test.
